steps.title: Use the contrast with Kuroda
steps.content: Kuroda adopted traditional European methods, but Hishida is associated with Nihonga, classical Japanese painting. The example of pigments on a silk scroll shows that Hishida used traditional Japanese approaches.

steps.title: Choose adoption over rejection
steps.content: A, C, and D all imply rejection, release, or neglect of traditional approaches, which contradicts the silk-scroll example. The blank needs a word meaning accepted and used. The answer is B.
